title: Dianthera ciliata
taxonomy: Dianthera ciliata is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flowers. Dianthera ciliata is further categorized as a species within the genus Dianthera. The genus Dianthera belongs to the order Lamiales, which includes plants like mint and sage. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Dianthera ciliata, also known as swamp loosestrife, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the wetlands of Central and South America. The health benefits of Dianthera ciliata include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight bacterial infections, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties.
